macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Global stocks rebound as chip shares lead gains, with S&P 500 up 0.9%
items.summary: World equities rallied after U.S. markets recovered, and the S&P 500 rose 0.9% as semiconductor stocks outperformed. Reuters also reported that the Dow climbed nearly 400 points, signaling a broad risk-on move after several sessions of losses.

items.title: Oil prices climb to five-week highs on U.S.-Iran conflict fears
items.summary: Crude rose as investors weighed escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities and the risk of supply disruptions in the Middle East. Reuters and Yahoo Finance both said oil prices hit fresh highs, with market concern centered on tighter fuel supplies and shipping routes.

items.title: Yen nears four-decade low as Japan signals readiness to intervene
items.summary: The Japanese yen remained near its weakest level in about 40 years, pressured by higher oil prices and U.S. Treasury yields. Japan's finance minister said Tokyo would take decisive action in the currency market if needed, raising the prospect of intervention.

items.title: UK inflation cools to 2.6% in June
items.summary: Britain's annual consumer price inflation fell to 2.6% from 2.8% in May, according to official data cited by Reuters. The softer reading is an important macro development for expectations around Bank of England policy.

items.title: U.S. House passes short-term funding bill to avert shutdown
items.summary: The Republican-controlled House approved a stopgap measure funding federal agencies through December 4, reducing near-term shutdown risk. Reuters said the bill was passed ahead of the November midterm election period.

items.title: Fed policy remains in focus as Waller warns rates could rise if inflation disappoints
items.summary: MarketWatch reported that Fed governor Christopher Waller said there is a chance the Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates soon if this week's inflation data comes in hot. That kept the policy outlook firmly in focus for bond and equity investors.

items.title: U.S. economic data and earnings season keep investors focused on growth and rates
items.summary: TradingEconomics' calendar flagged key U.S. releases including PMI, new home sales, GDP, core PCE, and employment-cost data, alongside multiple earnings beats. Reported upside surprises included General Motors, Northrop Grumman, 3M, Marsh & McLennan, and MSCI.

items.title: Tesla expected to make a major move after earnings
items.summary: CNBC said Tesla shares were poised for their biggest earnings-driven swing in a year ahead of the company's quarterly report. The setup made Tesla one of the most closely watched individual stocks in after-hours and next-day trading.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin climbs to multi-week highs on CLARITY Act optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin rose above $66,000 and hit a multi-week high as reports said the White House agreed to an ethics provision tied to the CLARITY Act, improving the odds of U.S. crypto market-structure legislation. Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin also posted gains alongside the move.

items.title: U.S. Senate momentum builds for the CLARITY Act
items.summary: Multiple reports said senators advanced the CLARITY Act and that the Senate was nearing a floor vote, with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent saying the bill was at the “one yard line.” The development was widely cited as a key catalyst for crypto market strength.

items.title: Pakistan launches crypto investigation unit to crack down on digital asset crimes
items.summary: Pakistan reportedly created a dedicated unit to investigate crypto-related crimes, signaling tighter enforcement around money laundering and other illicit activity. The move adds to a wave of regulatory scrutiny in the sector.

items.title: White House ethics deal advances crypto market-structure bill
items.summary: Cointelegraph reported that the White House agreed to ethics provisions in the market structure bill, helping clear one of the main political obstacles to the CLARITY Act. Bitcoin’s rally was tied directly to the legislative progress.

items.title: Bitcoin and crypto stocks rally ahead of Washington vote
items.summary: Bloomberg Crypto reported that crypto stocks were surging and that senators were targeting a floor vote on the CLARITY Act this week. The report said Bitcoin and Ether were higher as investors reacted to renewed legislative momentum.

items.title: Polygon Foundation says Ithaca hard fork deployed to Amoy testnet
items.summary: Polygon announced that the Ithaca hard fork has been deployed to the Amoy testnet and is scheduled for mainnet activation on July 30. The upgrade is one of the more notable project developments mentioned in the past day.

items.title: Ethereum developers prepare for Fusaka, second upgrade of 2025
items.summary: CoinDesk reported that Ethereum developers are preparing for Fusaka, the network’s second upgrade of 2025. The upgrade work is part of Ethereum’s continuing roadmap development.

items.title: Telegram to roll out native non-custodial Gram wallet
items.summary: Cointelegraph reported that Telegram founder Pavel Durov said the platform will launch a native non-custodial Gram wallet this summer. The rollout would bring self-custody crypto features to Telegram’s large user base.

items.title: Galaxy launches Bitcoin quantum computing initiative
items.summary: The Block reported that Galaxy launched a Bitcoin quantum computing initiative and will commit up to $5 million in developer grants. The program is intended to support research and development around Bitcoin security.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google releases new Gemini models and previews Gemini 4 training
items.summary: Google launched Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber, with a focus on lower latency, lower token costs, and cybersecurity use cases. Reuters and industry coverage also say Google did not give a launch date for Gemini 3.5 Pro and has begun pre-training Gemini 4.

items.title: Microsoft expands partnership with Mistral in Europe
items.summary: Microsoft said it will spend billions on Mistral’s computing infrastructure in Europe, expanding distribution of Mistral’s models through Microsoft’s cloud and software stack. Reports also say the deal includes new model access in Microsoft Foundry, Copilot Studio, and Azure Local.

items.title: AMD launches Helios, its first rack-scale AI system
items.summary: AMD unveiled Helios, a new AI rack system designed to compete with Nvidia and said Microsoft is a new customer. The system is part of AMD’s push to win more large-scale AI infrastructure deployments.

items.title: Anthropic copyright settlement approved
items.summary: A landmark $1.5 billion settlement resolving claims that Anthropic used copyrighted books to train its models without paying has been approved. Reuters says the settlement is among the most significant AI copyright cases so far.

items.title: OpenAI reportedly tied to Hugging Face security incident
items.summary: TechCrunch reported that OpenAI claimed responsibility for a Hugging Face breach, saying the issue arose from internal testing that went wrong. The incident adds to scrutiny of AI lab security practices and sandboxing.

items.title: Poolside ships Laguna S 2.1 coding model
items.summary: Poolside released Laguna S 2.1, a large open-weight coding model that it says outperforms DeepSeek V4-Pro Max on SWE-bench. The launch reflects continued competition in code-focused frontier models.

items.title: Alibaba and Moonshot AI add pressure in China’s AI race
items.summary: Coverage over the past day highlighted Alibaba’s new model preview and Moonshot AI’s Kimi update as signs that Chinese labs are closing the gap with U.S. frontier systems. AP and WSJ coverage also point to strong demand for Kimi K3 and broader concern about rapid progress from Chinese models.

items.title: Industry focus shifts toward AI infrastructure and data-center power demand
items.summary: Recent reporting says AI data centers could use far more electricity over the next decade, while suppliers and chipmakers continue expanding U.S. manufacturing and inference capacity. Reuters also noted Wistron’s new Texas facility for Nvidia systems and rising investment in AI infrastructure.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
